title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Stakeholder JavaScript Mission
summary: Residents using varied devices and assistive technology. People need keyboard-operable status filtering, updates, errors, and recovery without losing context. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Live Service Status Interface with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.
Propagate AbortSignal, deadlines, and cleanup through every cancellable asynchronous boundary.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — An accessible status filter has 5 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.
